roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.subtitle: System development roadmap and architecture overview
roadmap.architectureDesc: Current system infrastructure and technology stack

maturityDesc.experimental: Planned/exploratory stage, not yet production-ready and may change significantly.
maturityDesc.beta: Actively being built, partially usable but still evolving and subject to change.
maturityDesc.stable: Launched and tested — ready for production use.
maturityDesc.deprecated: No longer supported — a replacement feature is recommended.

roadmap.rulesDesc: Mandatory conventions — must not be violated in any feature

priority1: 5-Language i18n — all UI text via t('key') (th/en/cn/mm/jp)
Theme tokens — all theme-dependent styling from themeConfig
React Query — useQuery + invalidateQueries, no useEffect+setState
Table UX — TableSkeleton, sortable columns, ColumnSelector, pagination
priority2: Soft Delete — deletedAt + trash/restore, never hard delete
priority3: Low-Code Config — business rules via Admin UI, never hardcoded
priority4: Masterdata Fields — code @unique, name 5 languages, description, sortOrder, isActive
priority5: Kafka Event Standard — image.processed event for ERP services
imageService: Image Storage Rule — every image has raw + processed + thumbnail in image_files
Traceability Rule — every image traces to Camera → Machine → Lot → Operator → Shift
Event Rule — emit event on processing complete for downstream services

archStack.label: Capture Devices
archStack.desc: Windows PCs capture TIFF via SMB · 100+ cameras supported · Camera Health Monitor every 1 min · Status: active/inactive/error/maintenance

archStack.label: Sync Worker
archStack.desc: Recursive SMB scan · SHA256 checksum · Duplicate prevention · Priority Queue · Auto-stop polling on maintenance cameras

archStack.label: Processing Worker
archStack.desc: TIFF→PNG+Thumbnail · Pluggable Storage Client (S3/Local/SMB/NFS) · Provider refresh every 5 min · Horizontal scaling

archStack.label: API Service
archStack.desc: 25+ route modules · JWT + Refresh Token Rotation · RBAC on all endpoints · AES-256-GCM · Rate limit · Webhook HMAC-SHA256 · Audit Log · SSE

archStack.label: Frontend
archStack.desc: 22 lazy-loaded pages · 5 languages (TH/EN/CN/MM/JP) · Multi-theme · RBAC UI · Universal Search · Nav Bell · Bulk Delete + Trash Bin

archStack.label: Infrastructure
archStack.desc: Redis 7 · MinIO · SeaweedFS · Kafka 7.7 · HTTPS · Pluggable Storage Providers (S3/Local/SMB/NFS) · Storage Profiles + Migration Engine

archStack.label: Observability
archStack.desc: 24+ custom metrics · 4 Dashboards · Per-camera metrics · Image Renderer · Playlist auto-rotate · Log aggregation

roadmap.phase0Desc: End-to-end pipeline: Sync → Process → Store → Serve
phase0Tasks: image_files records created (7 images × 3 files = 21 records)
PNG + Thumbnail uploaded to MinIO (bucket: image-service)
File streaming download via GET /api/v1/images/:id/files/:fileType (200 OK)
Full pipeline verified: SMB → Sync → Process → MinIO → Serve → Download
Duplicate prevention via checksumMd5 lookup in sync-worker
Processing-logs API backend: CRUD, stats, retry endpoints
roadmap.phase1Desc: Recursive scan, SHA256 default, multi-camera scale
phase1Tasks: Recursive folder scanning — unlimited subfolder depth via smbclient ls recursion
SHA256 as default checksum algorithm (configurable via CHECKSUM_ALGORITHM env)
Auto-discovery of newly created folders during scan cycle
Support 30+ cameras with configurable poll intervals
phase2Tasks: Redis watermark — store lastScanned timestamp per camera for incremental scan
Persistent camera scan state in Redis (sync:camera:{id}:state hash)
Graceful recovery after worker restart — resume from last watermark
File move detection — track checksum + path changes
phase3Tasks: GET /api/v1/processing-logs (search, filter, paginate from ProcessingJob model)
GET /api/v1/processing-logs/stream SSE real-time push (stats + logs every 5s)
Processing Monitor dashboard with Live/Disconnected indicator + auto-refresh
Processing Logs page with search, status/type filter, sort, pagination
phase4Tasks: Login page with username/password form + validation + error handling
Axios JWT interceptor: auto-attach token, 401 → refresh → retry
Token refresh via POST /api/v1/auth/refresh + queue concurrent failed requests
Logout + user profile dropdown (username + role display)
roadmap.phase5Desc: File move detection, tag management, settings
phase5Tasks: File move detection — detect checksum + path changes across camera folders
Tag management in Image Detail modal (add, remove, filter by tag)
Settings Save to real backend / localStorage persistence
phase6Tasks: Kafka event emission on image.processed with full event model
Event model: imageId, cameraId, pngPath, thumbnailPath, capturedAt
roadmap.phase7Desc: Additional UI pages for existing data models
roadmap.phase8Desc: Multi-channel notifications and secure API key management
phase8Tasks: Telegram / Line notification channels with Alert Rules engine
Alert cooldown mechanism to prevent spam
API Key management with token generation and permission scoping
API Keys Management page with create / revoke / expiry
roadmap.phase9Desc: Low-code configurable business rules and masterdata entities
phase9Tasks: Admin UI for business rule thresholds (retention, compression, polling)
Masterdata entities: Camera Type, Image Category, Defect Type, Inspection Type
5-language name fields for all masterdata entities
roadmap.phase10Desc: Automated retention sweeper for expired TIFF/PNG/Thumbnail
phase10Tasks: Set retentionUntil on image ingestion based on camera's retention policy
Daily cron job to find and delete expired images from MinIO + DB
roadmap.phase11Desc: Dashboard with days-until-full projection for capacity planning
phase11Tasks: Storage capacity config (total limit per camera or globally)
Forecast calculation using growth trend data
Dashboard widgets: X days until full at 120/240/360 days
roadmap.phase12Desc: Formal DLQ monitoring dashboard and auto-reprocess
phase12Tasks: DLQ monitoring dashboard with retry/reject actions
Auto-reprocess scheduling for dead letter jobs
roadmap.phase13Desc: Full audit logging for all user actions and data changes
phase13Tasks: Audit logging on image delete, tag change, policy change, download
roadmap.phase14Desc: Automated restore testing and backup integrity checks
roadmap.phase15Desc: StorageProvider Interface, S3 (MinIO, SeaweedFS), LocalDisk with sharding, StorageRouter, worker upload refactor
phase15Tasks: S3Provider — wraps existing MinIO/SeaweedFS client, works with any S3-compatible storage
StorageRouter — in-memory registry, getDefault(), refresh() on mutation
Refactor MinIO calls in images, retention, admin, backup, health → StorageRouter
Worker writes directly to provider, sends metadata-only to API
Prisma: storage_providers table, image_files.storageProviderId + storageProfileId + checksumSha256
Auto-seed 'Primary Storage' from env on first deploy
roadmap.phase16Desc: Admin UI for Storage Providers, Migration Engine, Health Monitoring
phase16Tasks: Storage Providers CRUD page — add/edit/delete providers from UI
Test Connection — validates provider endpoint + credentials
Set Default Provider — changes where new files go
Provider Health Dashboard — capacity bar, latency, status per provider
Migration Engine — move data between providers via BullMQ background job
roadmap.phase17Desc: Policy-based routing to different storage backends per use case
phase17Tasks: Storage Profiles — Profile entity with provider routing rules
Policy-based routing — Default, Reject Archive, AI Dataset, OCR Archive
roadmap.phase18Desc: Direct NAS support via SMB, NFS, and SeaweedFS (S3-compatible) storage backends
phase18Tasks: SMBProvider — direct write to NAS via SMB protocol
NFSProvider — direct write to NAS via NFS mount
SeaweedFSProvider — uses existing S3Provider (S3-compatible API, zero config change)
Extends StorageProvider interface — zero business logic changes
roadmap.phase19Desc: Show real capacity per Storage Provider — Server local disk, MinIO/S3, NAS automatically with manual fallback for NAS
phase19Tasks: ✅ S3/MinIO — auto-fetch bucket size + capacity from API (fallback capacityBytes from DB)
✅ Local Disk — read real disk space via statfsSync (total/used/free)
✅ NAS (SMB/NFS) — manual capacity setting per Storage Provider (capacityBytes field)
✅ UI Dashboard — capacity bar per provider: name, type, used/total, %
✅ Alert when storage exceeds threshold (warning 75% / critical 90%)
✅ System config storage limit as fallback when auto-detection unavailable
roadmap.phase20Desc: Monitoring and Log Aggregation for 30-100+ cameras
phase20Tasks: ✅ Per-Camera Metrics — status, images, last poll per camera
✅ System Metrics — CPU, RAM, disk, DB connections
roadmap.phase21Desc: Flow diagram: Camera → Sync → Queue → Worker → Storage → DB with real-time status
phase21Tasks: ✅ Pipeline Flow Diagram with green/red status
roadmap.phase22Desc: Extended FileType enum for future AI processing + version chain
phase22Tasks: ✅ Extended FileType — ai, ocr, face_blur, custom
roadmap.phase23Desc: Auto-check camera status every 1 minute, alert on offline, track downtime
phase23Tasks: ✅ Camera Health Monitor — cron every 1 minute
✅ Auto Resolve when camera back online
roadmap.phase24Desc: Fix security vulnerabilities — encrypt SMB passwords, remove default secrets, auth on metrics
phase24Tasks: ✅ AES-256-GCM encryption for SMB passwords (using ENCRYPTION_KEY)
✅ WARNING log for default JWT_SECRET / ENCRYPTION_KEY in production
✅ Auth on /metrics endpoint (Prometheus UA / Service API Key / JWT)
✅ CORS origin: true for LAN — restrict in production
✅ Security headers: HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy
roadmap.phase25Desc: Optimize for 100+ cameras — Frontend code-splitting, Worker plugin, Migration batching
phase25Tasks: ✅ Frontend Code-Splitting — React.lazy() for all 22 pages
✅ Worker Provider Refresh — fetches config from API every 5 minutes
✅ Reduced getStreamData from 11 queries to 3 (80% latency reduction)
roadmap.phase26Desc: External system integration — Webhook, Multi-tenancy, Priority Queue
phase26Tasks: ✅ Webhook Callback — POST + HMAC-SHA256 on image.processed / camera.status_changed
✅ Priority Queue — high priority reprocess jumps to front
✅ Webhook Config — set webhook_url + webhook_secret from System Settings
roadmap.phase27Desc: External access via Cloudflare Tunnel — no open ports on firewall, auto SSL, WAF, DDoS protection
phase27Tasks: ✅ Created Tunnel connected to Cloudflare (BKK + SIN)
✅ Cloudflare Auto SSL + WAF + DDoS Protection
✅ No inbound ports on Firewall — outbound HTTPS only
✅ DNS-01 Challenge + Certbot — issued real Let's Encrypt SSL certificate for direct LAN access (HTTPS)
roadmap.phase28Desc: Camera availability analytics — Availability, MTBF, MTTR, Health Score, Downtime Report, Incident Management
phase28Tasks: ✅ Incident Management — Status Change Dialog + Resolution Dialog
✅ Dynamic Masterdata — all dropdowns managed from UI (9 types)
✅ Seeded 71 masterdata records across 9 types in 5 languages
✅ Connected camera_type + capture_mode to camera form
roadmap.phase29: P20 — Camera Incident Center & Knowledge Base
roadmap.phase29Desc: Central camera incident hub — search, filter, detail view, plus a knowledge base analyzing problems and resolutions
phase29Tasks: ✅ Related incidents (same reason or root cause)
✅ Knowledge base — incident totals, average MTTR
roadmap.phase30Desc: Create and track maintenance work orders linked to incidents
roadmap.phase32Desc: Predict camera failures ahead of time using AI
roadmap.phase33Desc: Reusable camera templates with per-camera overrides and multi-format ingestion
phase33Tasks: ✅ Camera templates with CRUD UI and default template
✅ Per-camera override of processing settings (blank = inherit)
✅ Optional PNG conversion with keep-smaller logic
✅ Optional thumbnail generation; raw always stored
✅ Flexible processing worker honoring resolved per-camera config
roadmap.phase34Desc: Add solution articles (Symptoms/Cause/Resolution/Verification/Tags) and link them to related incidents
phase34Tasks: Search and filter articles by tags/symptoms
roadmap.phase35Desc: Link work procedures to root causes (masterdata reason) and show step-by-step checklist guidelines for technicians
phase35Tasks: SOP step checklist definition per reason
Interactive SOP checkboxes in Resolution dialog
Track SOP checklist completion in incident logs
roadmap.phase36Desc: Display Experimental/Beta/Stable/Enterprise/Deprecated tags on the development roadmap
phase36Tasks: Add tooltip explanations for each status
roadmap.phase37Desc: Spare parts inventory management for cameras and related equipment
phase37Tasks: Spare parts catalog and inventory tracking
Link spare parts used to work orders
Reorder alerts when stock falls below safety limit
roadmap.phase38Desc: Track camera lifetime history, usage age, and preventive maintenance plans
roadmap.phase39Desc: Analyze maintenance costs combined with estimated lost image cost
phase39Tasks: Add repair cost field to incidents/work orders
ROI analysis comparing prevention vs repair
roadmap.phase40Desc: Rank camera reliability based on performance and uptime metrics
roadmap.phase41Desc: Consolidated dashboard showing performance metrics and camera statuses by department/plant
roadmap.phase42Desc: Link images to machine, production lot, order number, operator, and shift for full traceability
phase42Tasks: Extend Image model with production metadata fields
Integrate API ingestion to receive lot/order info
roadmap.phase43Desc: Enable event-driven integrations via Kafka for independent processing by external systems
phase43Tasks: Define formal event schema contract for image.processed
Implement Kafka producer publishing to event bus
roadmap.phase44: P26 — Data Lifecycle Policy & Auto Cleanup (Data Lifecycle Policy)
roadmap.phase44Desc: Define flexible storage capacity management, thresholds, and auto-cleanup policies for enterprise environments.
phase44Tasks: 4 Cleanup Modes: Retention Only, High Watermark, Circular Storage, Read Only
Image Locking protection to prevent deletion of audit files
Emergency Manual Cleanup modal with confirmation prompts
roadmap.phase45: P27 — Multi-Tier Storage Archiving (Multi-Tier Storage Archiving)
roadmap.phase45Desc: Automatically migrate files between high-speed local disk (Fast SSD) and cost-effective cold storage (Cold NAS/S3/Tape) before cleanup.
phase45Tasks: Integrate and categorize storage tiers (Hot, Cold, Archive)
Configure auto-migration policies to transfer files before deletion
Implement data rehydration when files are requested for viewing
roadmap.phase46: P37 — Vision AI Platform Integration (Vision AI Integration)
roadmap.phase46Desc: Convert processing workers into an AI Platform supporting Defect Detection, OCR, Object Detection, and Segmentation.
phase46Tasks: Model routing based on camera type and production part
Real-time defect classification using Deep Learning models
Optical Character Recognition (OCR) to read barcode/serial texts
Direct signaling to PLC arms for automatic defect sorting
roadmap.phase47: P38 — Video Formats & Live Streaming Support (Video Support)
roadmap.phase47Desc: Extend ingestion pipeline to support video files (MP4) and live video feeds (RTSP, HLS) directly.
phase47Tasks: Segment and archive continuous video recording feeds
Low-latency live streaming video player in user interface
Automatic keyframe extraction for pipeline AI analysis
roadmap.phase48: P39 — Edge Agent for Unstable Networks (Edge Agent)
roadmap.phase48Desc: Local-first Edge Agent software to compress, encrypt, and buffer image files when plant network is unstable.
phase48Tasks: Compress and encrypt image files at the edge before transmission
Local queue buffering on edge machines when backend is offline
Auto-resynchronization when network connectivity is restored
roadmap.phase49: P40 — Multi-Site Federation & HQ Dashboard (Multi-Site Federation)
roadmap.phase49Desc: Federate separate site deployments into a centralized HQ Dashboard for executives and cluster administrators.
phase49Tasks: Global dashboard comparing operational metrics across all sites
Share storage profiles and AI inference models across plants
Real-time health monitoring of all system servers nationwide
roadmap.phase50Desc: Generate ready-to-print audit reports compliant with strict manufacturing standards (ISO9001, FDA, GMP).
phase50Tasks: Printable audit trails and historical camera maintenance records
Historical data logs for pharmaceutical/food compliance rules
Proactive quality deviation alerts according to standards
roadmap.phase51: P42 — Multi-Language SDKs & Public API (SDK & Public API)
roadmap.phase51Desc: Enable external developers to integrate with the platform using official SDKs (Java, .NET, Python, Go, Node).
phase51Tasks: Build official SDK client libraries for popular languages
Interactive API documentation using Swagger / OpenAPI specs
Enhance Webhooks infrastructure for high-throughput events
roadmap.phase52: P43 — Mobile App for Technicians (Mobile App)
roadmap.phase52Desc: Empower technicians to scan QR codes on cameras/machines to view history, report failure symptoms, and submit repair reports.
phase52Tasks: Scan QR codes to inspect cameras, view history and incidents
Capture and upload failure evidence pictures from mobile camera
Receive urgent system warning alerts via Push Notifications
roadmap.phase53: P44 — 3D Factory Digital Twin Integration (Digital Twin)
roadmap.phase53Desc: Map camera locations onto a 3D plant layout, allowing administrators to visually navigate connected workstations.
phase53Tasks: Integrate interactive 3D plant layouts with live image assets
Click any machine element to open related camera feeds
Visualize camera field of view (FOV) projections on 3D maps
roadmap.phase54Desc: Download and share popular processing plugins like YOLO models, OCR text readers, or OpenCV privacy filters.
phase54Tasks: App-store style graphical UI to browse and install plugins
Secure execution sandbox for running downloaded plugins
Developer Portal for publishing custom analytics extensions
roadmap.phase55Desc: Ask questions about system metrics, failures, and statistics in plain natural language chats.
phase55Tasks: Analytical chatbot replying to diagnostics queries
Automatically generate and return charts in conversation threads
Predict and identify recurring root causes of failure trends
roadmap.phase56: P47 — Smart Factory Command Center (Command Center)
roadmap.phase56Desc: A unified command center dashboard integrating Image analytics, Machine status, OEE, Maintenance, and Energy consumption.
phase56Tasks: Integrate Image Service APIs with external ERP/MES/SCADA
Custom dashboards designed for high-resolution control rooms
Consolidated reporting comparing product quality with energy usage

docs.title: Product Documentation
docs.subtitle: Enterprise-grade developer portal, architecture breakdown, deployment instructions, and specifications.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.p1: Welcome to the Image Service Documentation Portal. The Image Service is an enterprise-grade platform designed for automated capturing, processing, archiving, and monitoring of high-resolution camera feeds inside manufacturing execution systems (MES) and enterprise resource planning (ERP) environments.
gettingStarted.noteBody: The system runs decoupled using workers communicating via a Redis BullMQ message queue. Cameras upload images to network SMB folders, which are pulled, converted, indexed, and pushed to S3 object storage safely.
gettingStarted.preItem1: Docker Engine v20.10+ & Docker Compose v2.0+
gettingStarted.preItem4: Network access to target camera shares (SMB/CIFS protocol)
gettingStarted.dockerBody: Run the docker container commands to fetch the workspace codebase and spin up:
gettingStarted.dockerWarning: Default superuser account is admin with password admin123. Change these credentials immediately upon deployment.
gettingStarted.scaleBody: If the number of cameras increases in the future and you need to process images faster, you can scale easily via Docker Compose without modifying any code:
gettingStarted.scaleStep1Desc: *This utilizes the remaining CPU headroom (~50%) and increases throughput to 3 - 4 images per second immediately.*
gettingStarted.scaleStep2: 2. Use Solid State Drives (SSD/NVMe) for Temporary Storage:
gettingStarted.scaleStep2Desc: Since the pipeline performs active file writes and uploads, deploying the app on SSD eliminates disk I/O latency bottlenecks.

architecture.title: System Architecture
architecture.p1: The Image Service follows a microservices pattern designed to process thousands of high-resolution industrial images per hour without throttling.
architecture.flow1Body: The Sync Worker polls cameras via SMB protocol. It scans for new TIFF/RAW files since the last checkpoint, downloads them to temporary storage, and posts job metadata to the Redis Queue.
architecture.flow2Body: The processing workers pull jobs asynchronously. They calculate the SHA-256 hash (preventing duplicate processing), extract image dimensions/EXIF metadata, convert RAW images to PNG, and save highly compressed thumbnails.
architecture.flow3Body: Files are uploaded to a configured Storage Provider (SeaweedFS, MinIO, or Local Directory). Simultaneously, metadata indices are written back to PostgreSQL via the API server and emit Webhook/WebSockets.
architecture.warnBody: For heavy loads, configure multiple replicas of the image-processing-worker to achieve high parallelization and load balance.

deployment.title: Deployment Guide
deployment.p1: Recommended deployment instructions for VM and production servers.
deployment.composeSub: Save the following content to a docker-compose.yml file and configure environment variables:
deployment.sslBody: To guarantee secure transmission of SMB credentials and API keys in enterprise production networks, HTTPS is enforced. Generates certificates using:

apiReference.title: API Reference
apiReference.p1: All write operations inside the API require Authentication and the corresponding Module Role Permission. Include the Bearer token in the header.
apiReference.createSub: Create a new camera and configure its network share via REST endpoint:

eventCatalog.title: Event Catalog
eventCatalog.p1: The Image Service publishes events to Webhooks, WebSockets, and Kafka when operations complete. Integrators can subscribe to these topics to build custom notifications.
eventCatalog.triggerSub: Triggered automatically when a worker finishes checksum calculation, conversion, and uploads to storage successfully:
eventCatalog.signingBody: To guarantee payloads have not been modified, Webhook headers include X-Image-Service-Signature using a pre-shared secret key hash (HMAC-SHA256).

pluginSdk.title: Worker & Plugin SDK
pluginSdk.p1: The Image Processing Worker can be customized to perform real-time AI inspections, object detection, or face blurring on images before archiving.
pluginSdk.hookSub: Implement a function inside the worker codebase to process downloaded files:
pluginSdk.tipBody: The plugin SDK runs on lightweight worker instances. Scale them horizontally to avoid interference with the main web portal response times.

databaseSchema.title: Database Schema
databaseSchema.p1: The database schema uses PostgreSQL. Below is the main model representation in the Prisma configuration format:
databaseSchema.entityCamera: Camera: Camera details, SMB paths, status.
databaseSchema.entityImage: ImageFile: Image records, size, status, file references.
databaseSchema.entityAudit: AuditLog: System actions, IPs, user modifications history.

storageProviders.title: Storage Providers
storageProviders.p1: Image Service supports multiple storage backends. Change providers dynamically from the UI settings.
storageProviders.localBody: Simple file mounts. Best for local development and direct VM hosting.
storageProviders.minioBody: Enterprise standard object storage. Supports cloud hosting and cluster nodes.
storageProviders.seaweedBody: Ultra-fast distributed storage optimized for billions of small images.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.p1: Quick diagnostic steps for operational issues.
troubleshooting.cameraSteps:
1. Verify the physical camera IP is pingable from the host machine VM.
2. Test the connection in the Camera Settings dialog to verify SMB credentials.
3. Ensure user account passwords are correct and have not expired in active directory.
troubleshooting.queueTitle: BullMQ queue length is piling up
troubleshooting.queueBody: If the queue wait list is growing rapidly, check processing worker container logs or scale workers.

bestPractices.title: Best Practices
bestPractices.securityItem1: Change default secrets (JWT_SECRET, ENCRYPTION_KEY) before staging.
bestPractices.securityItem2: Restrict backend access using firewalls; only allow scrapers on the metrics endpoint.
bestPractices.securityItem3: Establish separate API keys for external applications; do not share JWT tokens.

releaseNotes.title: Release Notes
releaseNotes.v110Body:
- Added 5-language internationalization support.
- Integrated system verification end-to-end API checking tool.
- Added camera soft-delete recycle bin mechanism with password confirmation logs.
releaseNotes.v100Body: - Initial production build release with SMB polling, worker queue management, and SeaweedFS/MinIO options.

roadmap.title: Product Roadmap
roadmap.p1: Upcoming development plan details aligned with core development phases:
roadmap.phase1Body: Integrate real-time anomaly detection, pattern matching, and OCR hooks directly into processing workers.
roadmap.phase2Body: Leverage AI anomaly scoring to predict camera lens degradation, focus errors, or connectivity drops.
roadmap.phase3Body: Fully synchronize metadata indexes directly into standard SAP/Oracle MES tracking lots systems.

userGuide.title: User & Dashboard Guide
userGuide.p1: Detailed guide to understanding metrics, system settings, and data visualizations across the dashboards.
userGuide.analyticsBody:
Details of the metrics and analysis criteria for camera health:

• Availability
  - Definition: The ratio of active camera operational time compared to total time in the period.
  - Recommendation: Should be greater than or equal to 99.5% (SLA Target).
  - Indication: Values below the target suggest frequent camera dropouts.

• MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures)
  - Definition: Average operational time between connection drops or errors (in hours).
  - Recommendation: The higher the better (ideally greater than 168 hours or 7 days).
  - Indication: Represents hardware and networking connection stability.

• MTTR (Mean Time To Repair)
  - Definition: Average repair or reconnect time since camera goes offline (in minutes).
  - Recommendation: The lower the better (ideally less than 15 minutes).
  - Indication: Represents the responsiveness of technician repairs or auto-reconnect loops.

• Health Score
  - Definition: A weighted index out of 100 calculated from Availability, MTBF, MTTR, and failure counts.
  - Recommendation:
    - 80 - 100: Excellent/Good (Green)
    - 50 - 79: Warning (Yellow)
    - Below 50: Critical (Red)

• Estimated Missed Images
  - Definition: The estimated count of image files lost during camera downtime.
  - Formula: Average images taken per hour when active * Total hours offline.
  - Indication: Helps Quality Assurance (QA) assess the scale of manufacturing data gaps.
userGuide.monitorBody:
In the Processing Monitor page, you can toggle between two modes in the top-right corner of the Job Status card:

• Real-time Mode (Last 20)
  - Purpose: Acts as a live heart rate monitor showing status metrics for only the last 20 jobs.
  - Indication: Best used to verify current throughput and immediately identify active ingestion errors.

• Cumulative Mode
  - Purpose: Shows total aggregate counts of all processing jobs recorded in the database.
  - Indication: Best used for historical reporting and long-term system load diagnostics.

storageGuide.introBody:
The system supports several storage backends. The 3 most common cases are Local Disk, MinIO (S3 Compatible), and SeaweedFS. Configure them under "Settings > Storage Providers".

Basic steps (apply to every type):
1. Go to the Storage Providers page and click "New Provider".
2. Enter a Provider Name - required, and must be unique among existing providers.
3. Choose a Provider Type: Local File System, S3 Compatible, SeaweedFS (S3-compatible), SMB Share, or NFS Export.
4. Fill in the fields specific to that type (see the next pages for each case).
5. Click Test Connection to verify it before relying on it in production.
6. Check Is Default if this should become the system's default provider (the previous default is automatically unset).

Note: which provider a given image file lands on is decided by the system's Default Provider, or by whichever Storage Profile is linked to a provider (configured under the Storage Profiles menu) - it is not tied directly to a camera or a retention policy.
storageGuide.localBody:
Best for testing the system, or when the server machine already has enough disk space.

Setup steps:
1. Set Provider Type to "Local File System".
2. Fill in the Root Directory (basePath), e.g. /data/images - this is a path on the machine (or container) running image-api.
   - The folder is created automatically if it doesn't exist yet, but the OS user running image-api must have write permission to that location.
3. Max Capacity (GB) is optional - the system auto-detects the remaining disk space. Fill it in only if you want to override that value.
4. Click Test Connection - this only checks write permission on the folder, it does not perform a full end-to-end read/write test.

Caveat: if you change basePath later, files already stored under the old path are not moved automatically.
storageGuide.minioBody:
Best for systems that need to scale out and want enterprise-grade storage security.

Setup steps:
1. Set Provider Type to "S3 Compatible".
2. Fill in Endpoint, e.g. minio (hostname only, no http:// or port).
3. Fill in Port - default is 9000.
4. Fill in Bucket, e.g. images - this bucket must already exist in MinIO beforehand; the system does not create it automatically (Test Connection will fail if the bucket doesn't exist yet).
5. Fill in the MinIO Access Key and Secret Key.
6. Check Use SSL if HTTPS is enabled on the MinIO side.
7. Enter Max Capacity (GB) manually - MinIO capacity cannot be auto-detected, so this value is required for the usage % calculation and low-space alerts.
8. Click Test Connection to confirm the connection and that the bucket is visible.

Note: the Region field shown in the form currently has no effect on the backend - filling it in or leaving it blank makes no difference.
storageGuide.seaweedBody:
Best for systems with a huge number of small image files that need high speed and efficient storage usage.

Setup steps:
1. Set Provider Type to "SeaweedFS (S3-compatible)" - the system connects through SeaweedFS Filer's S3 Gateway, using the exact same fields as MinIO.
2. Fill in Endpoint, e.g. seaweedfs-filer.
3. Fill in Port - the conventional value is 8333 (just a suggested placeholder, not enforced).
4. Fill in Bucket, Access Key, and Secret Key the same way as MinIO - the bucket must also be created on the SeaweedFS side beforehand.
5. Enter Max Capacity (GB) manually, since it also cannot be auto-detected like MinIO.
6. Click Test Connection to confirm.

Note: technically SeaweedFS is implemented with the exact same code path as MinIO in this system - only the endpoint/port differ.
storageGuide.caveatsBody:
1. Secrets are not encrypted
   Access Key, Secret Key, and Password for every provider are stored as plaintext in the database, and the API returns the raw values (including pre-filling them into the Edit form). Any user with storage:read permission can see all credentials. Restrict access to this page to trusted admins only.

2. Test Connection does not check everything
   - Local: only checks write permission on the folder, not a full end-to-end read/write test.
   - MinIO/SeaweedFS: only checks whether the bucket exists.

3. A provider that already has files cannot be deleted, and there is currently no UI screen to migrate files off it
   Plan your storage provider choice carefully upfront, since changing it later is difficult.

4. The "Required Fields Settings" page for Storage Provider currently has no real effect on the form
   Today the create/edit Storage Provider form only requires the Name field - checking other fields as required on the Required Fields Settings page has no effect on this form.

5. File destination is decided only via Global Default + Storage Profile
   Fine-grained rules (e.g. "this camera always stores to this provider") are not yet supported through the UI, even though the underlying data model supports it.

requiredFields.title: Required Fields Configuration
requiredFields.subtitle: Select the data fields that users must provide (Required) for each system module
requiredFields.alwaysRequiredHint: Fields marked * are always required by the system.

common.name: Name
common.clearDataWarning: This will permanently delete ALL images, processing jobs, audit logs, Storage objects, and Redis queue data. Cameras, users, roles, and system configuration will NOT be affected. This action CANNOT be undone.
